summary_text: Cancer drugs primarily target receptor tyrosine kinases and immune checkpoints
Neurodegeneration involves amyloid processing, tau phosphorylation, and mitochondrial dysfunction
Immune checkpoint inhibitors block PD-1/PD-L1 axis to restore anti-tumor immunity
Metabolic syndrome genes converge on insulin signaling and lipid metabolism pathways
Kinase inhibitors target BCR-ABL, EGFR, and BRAF with high selectivity
Inflammatory genes regulate NF-kB signaling and cytokine production
Apoptosis regulation involves BCL-2 family members and caspase cascades
Angiogenesis module centers on VEGF signaling and endothelial cell proliferation
